name: lint-fix
description: Lint check: read RTL+filelist → run verilator → fix errors → fix warnings → report

tasks.detail:
Before running lint, verify all input files exist:
- [ ] <ip>/rtl/<ip>.sv — RTL source (written by rtl-gen)
- [ ] <ip>/list/<ip>.f — Filelist (written by rtl-gen); contains all RTL file paths for this IP
Read <ip>.f and confirm every file listed in it actually exists on disk.
If any file is missing: report which file and stop.
Gate: <ip>.sv and <ip>.f both confirmed present, all files in filelist exist.

tasks.content: [LINT] Run lint — get full error and warning list
tasks.detail:
Run:
  verilator --lint-only -Wall -f <ip>/list/<ip>.f
Create <ip>/lint/ folder if it does not exist.
Record:
- [ ] Total errors
- [ ] Total warnings
- [ ] Per-file breakdown
Do not fix anything yet — get the complete picture first.
Gate: Lint output captured.

tasks.content: [LINT] Fix all lint errors (0 errors target)
tasks.detail:
Fix each error in <ip>/rtl/<ip>.sv:
- [ ] Undeclared signal → add logic/wire declaration
- [ ] Multiple drivers → remove duplicate drivers
- [ ] Implicit net → add `default_nettype none at top; declare all signals
- [ ] Port width mismatch → fix port connection width
- [ ] Syntax error → fix in the relevant file
Re-run verilator after each fix group. Repeat until 0 errors.
Gate: verilator reports 0 errors before fixing warnings.

tasks.content: [LINT] Fix lint warnings (latch, width mismatch, unused)
tasks.detail:
Fix each warning in <ip>/rtl/<ip>.sv by priority:
- [ ] Latch inferred → add default assignment in all always_comb branches
- [ ] Width mismatch → add explicit bit-select or cast
- [ ] Unused signal → remove it, connect it to real logic, or intentionally consume it with a named internal sink only when architecturally required
- [ ] Undriven signal → add driver or tie off
- [ ] DECLFILENAME → rename/split modules so filename and module name match
Do not add `verilator lint_off`, `lint_on`, `-Wno-*`, or generic waiver comments during normal cleanup. A waiver is valid only when the SSOT contains a precise coding_rules.lint_waivers entry naming the warning code, file, signal, and rationale.
Gate: 0 warnings.

Run final DUT-only lint report from the project root:
  python3 ../brian_hw/common_ai_agent/workflow/lint/scripts/dut_lint_report.py <ip> --top <top_module>
- [ ] Confirm 0 errors
- [ ] Confirm 0 warnings
- [ ] Confirm <ip>/lint/dut_lint.json has dut_only=true, scope=dut, passed=true
Gate: Clean canonical lint run before writing report.

Write <ip>/lint/lint_report.txt with:
- [ ] Date and verilator version
- [ ] Input files from <ip>/list/<ip>.f
- [ ] Result: CLEAN only when dut_lint.json passed=true
- [ ] Total errors fixed: N
- [ ] Total warnings fixed: N
- [ ] Remaining SSOT waivers: list only entries present in coding_rules.lint_waivers
Gate: File written to <ip>/lint/lint_report.txt — verified by check_lint_disk.sh or dut_lint.json (0 errors / 0 warnings / passed=true).
